html:
A Pumpkin Cookie is a Food item cooked in a Crock Pot by combining 1 Pumpkin and 2 Honey (or Honeycombs). It takes 40 seconds to cook.

Using 3 Honey or Honeycombs may result in Taffy instead. Adding 1 Mandrake may result in Mandrake Soup.

Pumpkin Cookies restore the same amount of Sanity as Taffy, but lose hunger over its own ingredients, restoring the same hunger as a raw or cooked pumpkin while lacking the small health boost from either.

Prerequisites

  • Requires:

Recipe

×2

Examples

×2

 A recipe for Pumpkin Cookies that preserves as much hunger value as possible

×2

×3

50% chance to produce Taffy.

×2

50% chance to produce Mandrake Soup.

×2

Usage

×4

Tips

  • Practically, eating a Cooked Pumpkin alongside Taffy is more beneficial than eating Pumpkin Cookies. Since a Cooked Pumpkin restores 8 , it effectively cancels Taffy's Health penalty as well as restoring some Health back, while restoring more Hunger than a Pumpkin Cookie.
  • With 1 Pumpkin, 3 Honey, and 1 Twig, the Player can restore a total of 5 , 62.5 , and 15 from 1 Taffy and 1 Cooked Pumpkin, compared to 3 , 47, and 15 from 1 Pumpkin Cookie and 1 Honey.
  • However, if your honey reserves are running very low, then Pumpkin Cookies do provide more sanity per unit of honey, 15 per two honey vs Taffy's 15 per three honey.
  • It is inadvisable to use this recipe as a sanity source, as the sanity gain is the same as a single cooked green cap while costing at least 18.75 and 9 over its ingredients.

Trivia

  • Pumpkin Cookies were added in the Insanity! update.
  • During the Pig Fiesta event in the Hamlet DLC, Pumpkin Cookies take on a more festive appearance, resembling a fortune cookie.

Gallery

  • Original HD icon from Bonus Materials from CD Don't Starve.
  • Icon used as emoji for Official Klei Discord.
  • Pumpkin Cookies as seen in the poster for the Insanity! update.
By TypeBy Crock Pot Value

A Pumpkin Cookie is a Food item cooked in a Crock Pot by combining 1 Pumpkin and 2 Honey (or Honeycombs). It takes 40 seconds to cook.
Using 3 Honey or Honeycombs may result in Taffy instead. Adding 1 Mandrake may result in Mandrake Soup.
Pumpkin Cookies restore the same amount of Sanity as Taffy, but lose hunger over its own ingredients, restoring the same hunger as a raw or cooked pumpkin while lacking the small health boost from either.

Tips[edit | edit source]
Practically, eating a Cooked Pumpkin alongside Taffy is more beneficial than eating Pumpkin Cookies. Since a Cooked Pumpkin restores 8 , it effectively cancels Taffy's Health penalty as well as restoring some Health back, while restoring more Hunger than a Pumpkin Cookie.
With 1 Pumpkin, 3 Honey, and 1 Twig, the Player can restore a total of 5 , 62.5 , and 15 from 1 Taffy and 1 Cooked Pumpkin, compared to 3 , 47, and 15 from 1 Pumpkin Cookie and 1 Honey.
However, if your honey reserves are running very low, then Pumpkin Cookies do provide more sanity per unit of honey, 15 per two honey vs Taffy's 15 per three honey.
It is inadvisable to use this recipe as a sanity source, as the sanity gain is the same as a single cooked green cap while costing at least 18.75 and 9 over its ingredients.

Pumpkin Cookies were added in the Insanity! update.

A '''Pumpkin Cookie''' is a [[Food]] [[item]] cooked in a [[Crock Pot]] by combining 1 [[Pumpkin]] and 2 [[Honey]] (or [[Honeycomb]]s). It takes 40 seconds to cook.

Using 3 Honey or Honeycombs may result in [[Taffy]] instead. Adding 1 [[Mandrake]] may result in [[Mandrake Soup]].

Pumpkin Cookies restore the same amount of [[Sanity]] as Taffy, but lose hunger over its own ingredients, restoring the same hunger as a raw or cooked pumpkin while lacking the small health boost from either.

== Prerequisites ==
* Requires: [[File:Sweetener.png|link=Sweetener]] [[File:Pumpkins.png|link=Pumpkin]]

== {{pic|32|Cookbook}} Recipe ==
{{Recipe
|item1 = Pumpkins
|item2 = Sweetener
|count2 = 2
|item3 = Filler
|tool = Crock Pot
|result = Pumpkin Cookies
}}

== Examples ==
{{Recipe
|item1 = Pumpkin
|item2 = Honey
|count2 = 2
|item3 = Twigs
|tool = Crock Pot
|result = Pumpkin Cookies
|note={{Pic24|Prototype}} A recipe for Pumpkin Cookies that preserves as much hunger value as possible}}

{{Recipe
|item1 = Pumpkin
|item2 = Honeycomb
|count2 = 2
|item3 = Monster Meat
|tool = Crock Pot
|result = Pumpkin Cookies
}}

{{Recipe
|item1 = Pumpkin
|item2 = Honey
|count2 = 3
|tool = Crock Pot
|result = Pumpkin Cookies
|note = 50% chance to produce [[Taffy]].
}}

{{Recipe
|item1 = Pumpkins
|item2 = Sweetener
|count2 = 2
|item3 = Mandrake
|tool = Crock Pot
|result = Pumpkin Cookies
|note = 50% chance to produce [[Mandrake Soup]].
}}

{{Recipe
|item1 = Pumpkin
|item2 = Honey
|count2 = 2
|item3 = Ice
|tool = Crock Pot
|result = Pumpkin Cookies
|dlc = Reign of Giants
}}

== {{pic|32|Icon Tools}} Usage ==
{{Recipe
|item = Pumpkin Cookies
|tool = Pig Icon
|result = Manure
}}

{{Recipe
|item1 = Pumpkin Cookies
|tool = Pig Usher
|result = Oinc
|resultcount = 4
|dlc = Hamlet
}}

== {{Pic|32|Prototype}} Tips ==
* Practically, eating a Cooked Pumpkin alongside Taffy is more beneficial than eating Pumpkin Cookies. Since a Cooked Pumpkin restores 8 {{hp}}, it effectively cancels Taffy's Health penalty as well as restoring some Health back, while restoring more Hunger than a Pumpkin Cookie.
*With 1 Pumpkin, 3 Honey, and 1 Twig, the Player can restore a total of 5 {{Hp}}, 62.5 {{Hunger}}, and 15 {{Sanity}} from 1 Taffy and 1 Cooked Pumpkin, compared to 3 {{Hp}}, 47{{Hunger}}, and 15 {{Sanity}} from 1 Pumpkin Cookie and 1 Honey.
* However, if your honey reserves are running very low, then Pumpkin Cookies do provide more sanity per unit of honey, 15 {{Sanity}} per two honey vs Taffy's 15 {{Sanity}} per three honey.
*It is inadvisable to use this recipe as a sanity source, as the sanity gain is the same as a single [[Green Cap|cooked green cap]] while costing at least 18.75 {{Hunger}} and 9 {{Hp}} over its ingredients.

== {{pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia ==
* Pumpkin Cookies were added in the ''[[Don't Starve/Version History#Insanity!|Insanity!]]'' update.
* During the [[Pig Fiesta]] event in the ''[[Don't Starve: Hamlet|Hamlet]]'' DLC, Pumpkin Cookies take on a more festive appearance, resembling a fortune cookie.
